mechanism_narrative: Persistent growth in real GDP (GDPC1) signals strong economic demand, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods of GDPC1 growth remain below 1% despite sustained GDP expansion.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in UNRATE above 4.5% indicates deteriorating labor market conditions, reducing consumer spending confidence and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns remain positive in 70% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL signal accommodative monetary policy and disinflation, creating favorable equity valuation conditions that produce positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: 60-day SPX returns show negative correlation with combined FEDFUNDS/CPIAUCSL declines in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in INDPRO below 100 signals weakening industrial output, reducing corporate profitability and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain positive in 60% of INDPRO declines below 100.

mechanism_narrative: Rising RSAFS (consumer credit) combined with declining HOUST (housing starts) indicates weakening demand-side momentum, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns show positive correlation with simultaneous RSAFS increases and HOUST declines in 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in PAYEMS below 100k net job additions signals labor market weakness, reducing consumer spending and causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain positive in 50% of PAYEMS declines below 100k.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in FEDFUNDS above 4.5% during high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 3%) signals aggressive monetary tightening, creating headwinds for equity markets and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns show positive correlation with FEDFUNDS/CPIAUCSL dual increases in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in HOUST below 1,300k units indicates weakening housing demand, reducing construction activity and causing negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns remain positive in 60% of HOUST declines below 1,300k.
